Let $\lbrace a_n \rbrace_{n \ge 1}$ be an infinite sequence with $a_n \ge 0$
for all $n$.  For $n \ge 1$, let $b_n$ denote the geometric mean of $a_1,
\dots, a_n$, that is $(a_1 \dots a_n)^{1/n}$.

Suppose $\displaystyle{\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} a_n}$ is convergent. Prove that
$\displaystyle{\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} b_n}$ is also convergent.
